title: d2c IR Flow Graph
description: Flow-level IR emitted by d2c-build-flow Phase 2a before any per-page Phase 2 runs. Declares the ordered set of pages, linear edges between them, optional shared layouts, optional shared state, and not-supported detections (overlays, conditionals, loops). Authoritative source is the user's parsed prompt; Figma prototype metadata only feeds shell detection and contradiction warnings.

schema_version.description: Flow IR artifact schema version. v1 only in MVP.

mode.description: Resolved flow shape: 'routes' = every step is its own URL (original behavior); 'stepper' = all steps share one URL and swap in place inside a shared shell; 'hybrid' = one or more contiguous stepper groups intermixed with standalone routes. Populated by Phase 2a from the prompt's `mode:` directive, or by detect-mode.js when the directive was `auto` or omitted. Exactly one value; 'auto' is never stored — it is the parser-level default that triggers detection and gets replaced with one of the three terminal values.

mode_source.description: How `mode` was chosen. 'explicit' = user passed `mode: routes|stepper|hybrid` in the prompt. 'auto-detected' = user omitted `mode:` (or passed `mode: auto`) and detect-mode.js picked the terminal value based on Figma signals.

mode_confidence.description: Overall detector confidence in the chosen `mode`, in [0, 1]. Populated when mode_source='auto-detected'; null for 'explicit'. ≥0.80 → silent auto-pick; 0.55–0.80 → pick with advisory; <0.55 → Phase 2a aborts before the IR is written (so this field is never serialised below 0.55).

mode_detection_reasons.description: Ordered signal breakdown from detect-mode.js when mode_source='auto-detected'. Written to the Phase 2a report for operator inspection. Empty array when mode_source='explicit'.

flow_name.description: Routing slug parent (e.g. 'onboarding'). Inferred from base_route basename; STOP-AND-ASK when absent.

framework.description: Inherited from design-tokens.json. v1 codegen only supports react.

meta_framework.description: Inherited from design-tokens.json. v1 codegen only supports next.

base_route.description: Optional base route extracted from the prompt preamble (e.g. '/onboarding'). Null when the user provided explicit per-step routes and no preamble route.

auto_discovered.description: True when the flow was discovered from Figma prototype connections starting at entry_node_id rather than an explicit step list (Form C: `Step: <entry-url>`). When true, every edge MUST be inferred=false with a non-null source_component_node_id.

cross_file.description: True when pages[] spans more than one Figma file (B-FLOW-CROSS-FILE). Phase 2a must fetch metadata per unique file_key; the Phase 6 report surfaces the dependency list.

file_keys.description: Unique Figma file keys across pages[], in first-appearance order. Populated even for single-file flows (length 1).

entry_node_id.description: node_id of the first page (step 1). Must appear in pages[].

pages.description: Ordered list of pages. For mode='routes' one entry per parsed step (minimum 2). For mode='stepper' a single virtual page entry with page_type='stepper_group' standing in for the whole in-place flow (minimum 1, the group is expanded in stepper_groups[]). For mode='hybrid' one entry per standalone step plus one virtual page per stepper group. Order is authoritative; step_number is 1-based.

stepper_groups.description: Zero or more stepper groups. A stepper group is a contiguous run of Figma frames that share a single URL and swap in place (single-route multi-step). Exists alongside `pages[]`; each group is represented in pages[] by a single virtual entry with page_type='stepper_group' and the same node_id as stepper_groups[i].group_node_id. Empty for mode='routes'; one entry for mode='stepper'; one-or-more for mode='hybrid'.

edges.description: Ordered list of navigation edges. For linear flows, one edge per consecutive page pair (length = pages.length - 1). For branching flows (B-FLOW-MULTI-BRANCH), a page may have out-degree > 1 — one outgoing edge per branch — and every outgoing branch MUST carry a non-null source_component_node_id (validator-enforced).

layouts.description: Zero or more shared layout shells detected across pages. Empty when shell detection finds no common subtree above threshold.

shared_state.description: Zero or more shared-state declarations spanning multiple pages. Empty when no form-like shared state is declared or detected.

not_supported_detected.description: Overlay/conditional/loop detections that the user was informed about. Each entry was surfaced to the user before codegen.

mask_regions.description: Optional per-page rectangles to zero out before pixel-diff comparison (B-FLOW-NAV-TEST-MASKING). Used to hide auto-generated chrome (steppers, progress bars) that would otherwise produce false negatives. Consumed by pixeldiff.js via `--mask <regions.json>`.

step_number.description: 1-based step number from the user's prompt. Must equal order.

node_id.description: Figma node id extracted from the step URL (e.g. '1:234'). Unique across pages[].

figma_url.description: The exact URL the user supplied for this step.

file_key.description: Figma file key (the path segment after `/design/` or `/file/`). Enables cross-file flows (B-FLOW-CROSS-FILE); null when the URL could not be parsed.

title.description: Figma frame name fetched via get_design_context. Human-readable anchor for the report.

route.description: Resolved route: explicit per-step route wins, otherwise base_route + /step-<N>.

order.description: 1-based position in the flow. Equals step_number in v1.

layout.description: Name of the layouts[] entry this page renders inside, or null if no shared shell applies.

page_type.description: Discriminates full-page routes from overlay/modal children and stepper-group virtual pages. 'page' renders at its own URL; 'overlay' mounts inside its parent page via an overlay portal and reflects its open/closed state through a `?overlay=<id>` query param (B-FLOW-OVERLAY-SUPPORT); 'stepper_group' is a virtual page standing in for a single-route multi-step group — its real step frames live in stepper_groups[].steps.

stepper_group_ref.description: When page_type='stepper_group', the name of the stepper_groups[] entry this page represents. Validator enforces that it matches exactly one stepper_groups[].name. Null for 'page' and 'overlay'.

overlay_parent.description: When page_type='overlay', the node_id of the parent page that mounts this overlay. Must appear in pages[].node_id. Null for page_type='page'.

overlay_query_param.description: Query-param key used to signal the overlay's open state in the URL (default 'overlay'). Null for page_type='page'. The overlay is open when `?<param>=<overlay-slug>` matches the overlay's route slug.

branch.description: Optional branch letter for multi-branch flows (B-FLOW-MULTI-BRANCH). When set, this page is one of N siblings at the same step_number (e.g. 3a, 3b). Null for linear flows.

mobile_variant.description: Mobile viewport pair for this page (B-FLOW-MOBILE-VARIANT). When set, Phase 4 verifies both viewports and codegen emits responsive CSS (tokens-aware) rather than two components. Null for desktop-only pages.

figma_url.description: Exact mobile Figma frame URL supplied via `mobile: <url>`.

node_id.description: Mobile frame's Figma node id (colon form).

file_key.description: Figma file key of the mobile frame; may differ from the desktop file (rarely useful, but allowed).

state_writes.description: Fields this page writes into shared state, from the optional `state:` directive in Phase 1. Names feed the generated TypeScript interface; types restricted to primitives in v1.

name.description: camelCase field identifier (matches the shared-state key).

type.description: Primitive TypeScript type. Complex shapes are not supported in v1.

from_node_id.description: Source page node_id. Must appear in pages[].node_id. May repeat across edges[] entries when a page has multiple outgoing branches (B-FLOW-MULTI-BRANCH) or when a page opens multiple overlays (B-FLOW-OVERLAY-SUPPORT).

to_node_id.description: Destination page node_id. Must appear in pages[].node_id.

source_component_node_id.description: Figma node id of the button/link that triggers the navigation when identifiable. Null when the edge is inferred from step order and no wireable component was found.

inferred.description: True when the edge was derived from step order rather than a wired Figma prototype link or an identifiable Next button.

condition.description: Optional gating condition for the edge (B-FLOW-CONDITIONAL-NAV). Null means the edge always fires. When set, codegen wraps the navigation in a state-check — see the condition definition.

action.description: Edge action kind (B-FLOW-OVERLAY-SUPPORT). 'navigate' = router.push to the target page. 'open-overlay' = mount the target overlay inside its parent page and set ?overlay=<slug>. 'close-overlay' = clear the overlay query param. Defaults to 'navigate'.

is_loop.description: True when this edge closes a cycle in the flow graph (B-FLOW-LOOP-SUPPORT). The validator treats loop edges specially: cycles are legal, but per-test visit caps apply in the generated nav smoke test.

kind.description: Condition shape. 'state-equals' requires a matching `value`; 'state-truthy'/'state-falsy' coerce the field to Boolean.

field.description: Shared-state key to read. MUST match a key that some page writes via `state:` — validator flags unknown fields as likely typos.

value.description: Required when kind='state-equals'. Compared with strict equality against the shared-state value at nav time.

name.description: PascalCase identifier used for the generated layout component (e.g. 'OnboardingShell').

figma_node_id.description: Figma node id of the shared component when detected; null when the shell was inferred from overlap across pages.

applies_to.description: List of page node_ids that render inside this layout. Each must appear in pages[].node_id.

name.description: camelCase identifier for the shared state slice (e.g. 'onboardingData').

shape.description: Whether the state is a form (keyed field values) or an opaque object. v1 codegen treats both as a plain object with setters.

pages.description: Page node_ids that read or write this state. Each must appear in pages[].node_id.

persistence.description: Where the shared state lives between page navigations. 'memory' = component state (lost on reload); 'session' = sessionStorage (survives navigations, cleared on tab close); 'local' = localStorage (survives across sessions, cleared only on explicit reset or TTL expiry). Pair 'local' with an optional `ttl_seconds` to invalidate stored data after a time window.

ttl_seconds.description: Optional time-to-live for 'local' persistence, in seconds. When set, the provider wraps each write with a timestamp and discards stale data on read. Null (or absent) means 'never expire until reset() is called'. Ignored when persistence is 'memory' or 'session'.

node_id.description: Figma node id where the construct was detected.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation shown to the user before they confirmed to continue.

page_node_id.description: node_id of the page this mask applies to. Must appear in pages[].node_id.

rect.description: Axis-aligned rectangle, in pixel coordinates of the rendered screenshot. Both the Figma export and the generated screenshot must agree on the coordinate system — pixeldiff zeroes the rect on both sides before comparing.

reason.description: Human-readable justification (e.g. 'progress bar auto-generated by the stepper component').

stepper_group.description: A contiguous run of Figma frames rendered as a single-route multi-step stepper. The group lives at a single URL; step content swaps in place while a shared shell (header/progress/footer) stays mounted.

name.description: PascalCase identifier. Drives the generated component name (e.g. 'OnboardingStepper') and the state-provider slice (e.g. 'OnboardingStepperContext').

route.description: The single URL the stepper is mounted at. Must not collide with any pages[i].route whose page_type='page'.

group_node_id.description: Synthetic node_id used to represent this group as a single pages[] entry (format: 'stepper:<hash>'). pages[].node_id === stepper_groups[].group_node_id for the virtual page.

persistence.description: Where the stepper's `currentStep` index and shared form state live between step advances and between page reloads. Mirrors shared_state[].persistence.

ttl_seconds.description: Optional TTL when persistence='local'. Null = no expiry. Ignored for 'memory' and 'session'.

shell_component_node_id.description: Figma node id of the shared shell component that wraps the step body (header + progress indicator + footer). Null when detection found no shared shell — codegen emits the stepper without a wrapper in that case.

stepper_indicator_node_id.description: Figma node id of the stepper indicator sub-instance inside the shell (the progress dots / numbered bar whose variant advances per step). Null when no ordered-variant indicator was found; codegen falls back to a generic 'Step N of M' label in that case.

steps.description: Ordered steps inside the group. steps[i].order is 1-based and contiguous.

validation_enabled.description: True when any step has validate !== 'none'. When true, codegen emits a useReducer-based state machine instead of useState, so Next can be blocked until the current step validates.

branches.description: Forward-compatible placeholder for v2 branching stepper paths (e.g. step 2 → 3a or 3b). Always empty in v1; validator rejects non-empty values.

transition.description: Forward-compatible placeholder for v2 step transition animations. Only 'none' is legal in v1; the shared-shell design-system component owns any animation today.

detected_mode_run.description: True when this group was produced by detect-mode.js rather than an explicit `Stepper group` block in the prompt. Operators see this in the Phase 2a report.

order.description: 1-based position within the stepper_group.steps[] array. Must be contiguous starting at 1.

node_id.description: Figma node id of this step's frame. Unique across all stepper_groups[*].steps[*] AND pages[].

figma_url.description: The exact Figma URL for this step's frame.

file_key.description: Figma file key; null when the URL could not be parsed.

title.description: Human-readable step label (e.g. 'Email'). Defaults to the Figma frame name when `title:` was not supplied.

optional.description: When true, the stepper exposes a Skip button; codegen wires it to advance without running validation.

validate.description: What the generated Next button must satisfy before advancing. 'none' = always enabled. 'form' = all fields in the step's form must be valid (validity is tracked in the stepper provider).

state_writes.description: Fields this step writes into the stepper's shared state. Same shape as pages[].state_writes.

detection_confidence.description: Per-step confidence score when the step was auto-assigned to this group by detect-mode.js. Null for explicit groups.

mode_detection_reason.description: Single signal entry from detect-mode.js. Written to mode_detection_reasons[] in order of contribution.

signal.description: Which of the five canonical signals produced this entry.

score.description: Contribution of this signal to the overall mode confidence, in [0, 1].

detail.description: Short human-readable explanation (e.g. 'all 4 frames 1440×900 ±0px', 'indicator variant step=1→4 across frames').
